Name: curvatureawareprecisionrouting
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient and Stable Deep Learning Training
Short Hypothesis: Introducing a curvature-aware precision routing framework that dynamically adjusts precision levels based on the curvature of the loss landscape can lead to more efficient and stable training of deep neural networks while maintaining or improving model performance.
Related Work: Previous work in deep learning optimization has explored the use of mixed-precision training and curvature-based methods to improve training efficiency and stability. However, most approaches treat precision levels as static or use simple heuristics. This proposal builds on recent advancements in curvature-aware optimization and mixed-precision training by introducing a dynamic, curvature-aware precision routing mechanism. Unlike existing methods, our approach formalizes the relationship between curvature and precision, providing a theoretically grounded and empirically validated framework for adaptive precision adjustments.
Abstract: We propose a curvature-aware precision routing (CPR) framework for deep neural networks. CPR dynamically adjusts the precision levels of model parameters based on the curvature of the loss landscape, aiming to balance computational efficiency and numerical stability. By leveraging geometric subspace diagnostics and deterministic spectral estimators, CPR quantifies gradient covariance decay and Hessian conditioning to guide precision switching. This approach ensures that high-curvature regions, where numerical stability is critical, use higher precision, while low-curvature regions benefit from the efficiency of lower precision. We validate CPR through a series of experiments on standard benchmarks, demonstrating improvements in training time and model accuracy compared to static mixed-precision methods.
Experiments: Training a ResNet-18 on CIFAR-10 with CPR, comparing it to static mixed-precision and full-precision training in terms of training time, memory usage, and final accuracy.
Evaluating the impact of CPR on the stability of training by measuring the condition number of the Hessian and gradient covariance over training epochs.
Performing ablation studies to isolate the effects of curvature-based precision switching from other hyperparameters, such as learning rate and batch size.
Comparing CPR against state-of-the-art curvature-aware optimization methods, such as AdamW and LARS, on a range of deep learning tasks including image classification and language modeling.
Conducting multi-seed experiments to quantify the variance in performance and ensure the robustness of CPR.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The dynamic adjustment of precision levels may introduce additional overhead, which could offset some of the efficiency gains.
The curvature estimation and precision switching mechanisms may be sensitive to hyperparameters, requiring careful tuning for optimal performance.
The framework's effectiveness may vary across different architectures and datasets, necessitating extensive empirical validation.
The theoretical analysis of curvature-aware precision routing may be complex, requiring advanced mathematical tools and assumptions.
